mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, triggering a positive earnings revision cycle that lifts the S&P 500 within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and residential housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion. This dual demand surge compresses credit spreads and drives institutional capital allocation toward equities, boosting 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates capacity utilization is expanding without triggering inflationary bottlenecks. This supply-driven earnings growth sustains positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above 115 while CPIAUCSL increases by more than 2.5 percent annually, indicating supply constraints are overriding growth benefits.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive policy tightening. This compresses equity risk premiums and forces valuation multiple contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in one month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX changes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales reflects resilient consumer demand. This cash flow visibility supports near-term equity earnings estimates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference between positive and negative RSAFS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market recovery. This dual improvement reduces household precautionary savings and fuels durable goods demand, lifting quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 60-day returns across three consecutive economic cycles, or the interaction coefficient becomes negative during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.5 million annualized units during a low-interest-rate environment trigger a construction multiplier effect. This demand shock propagates through supply chains and boosts corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where HOUST exceeds the threshold while CPIAUCSL rises above 3.0 percent, indicating inflation dominates the housing multiplier.
